title: Sanji Holds Back the Fight
description: On pages 9–12, sanji holds back the fight as luffy refuses to fight the wildlife while carrying Nami, and the journey toward the doctor becomes a brutal climb through Drum’s snowfields.
scene: Sanji Holds Back the Fight. Luffy refuses to fight the wildlife while carrying Nami, and the journey toward the doctor becomes a brutal climb through Drum’s snowfields. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hey Sanji, we can climb up from here!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Just keep running all the way to the top!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hmph... to think they'd put up watchguards in the short time I was gone...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Opening fire on us? What fools...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Ahh... those grand, majestic mountains towering over all else as always!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: It appears to be in good shape, your majesty.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Then let us be off at once! Back to the castle!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's time to stop being a pirate and return to my life as a king!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: What's the matter with you!? Are you still angry that I killed your brother?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: I'll give you a nice reward soon enough,

summary: This part centers on sanji holds back the fight, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
